mappings.notes: Injecting malicious data into training pipelines to corrupt model behaviour

Reconstructing sensitive training data from model outputs

mappings.control_id: High-risk AI must be resilient to attempts to alter performance through data manipulation

Training pipeline dependency scanning — vulnerabilities in training infrastructure components

Training data quality requirements include integrity — anomaly detection, source allowlisting, provenance tracking as data quality controls
Training data provenance documented — full chain from source to training dataset, modification history tracked
Training pipeline integrity controls — input validation, source allowlisting as AIMS security design requirements
Poisoning detection in AIMS testing — backdoor trigger testing, biased output detection before each production promotion
Automated scanning of training pipeline components — vulnerabilities in data processing libraries
Hardened training pipeline configurations — immutable infrastructure, locked data sources
Poisoning scenarios in penetration testing — verify data integrity controls hold under attack
Full audit trail of training data provenance and model training runs

Root cause analysis for poisoning incidents — identify affected deployments, assess physical impact

Automated monitoring of LLM outputs in CDE — poisoning indicators (systematic anomalous recommendations) detected
Poisoning detection included in penetration testing programme for LLM applications in CDE
Targeted risk analysis for training pipeline security — documented controls and frequency of review
Training data integrity controls — source allowlisting, anomaly detection, provenance tracking as DMS practices
Post-training backdoor detection and model integrity verification as ASI practices before deployment
Production monitoring for poisoning indicators — systematic output anomalies detected through AI-specific monitoring
Model rollback and poisoning incident response as ENISA IRS practice for AI-specific incidents

mappings.control_id: Write access to training data stores enables poisoning
mappings.notes: Read-only credentials for data consumption; separate write credentials with MFA

mappings.control_id: Third-party data pipeline credentials with training data write access
mappings.notes: Apply NHI-3 controls to all data pipeline third-party credentials

mappings.control_name: Design software — resource and availability constraints
mappings.notes: Include resource limits, rate limiting, and availability requirements as explicit security design requirements for AI inference services

mappings.control_name: Test for security vulnerabilities — load and adversarial testing
mappings.notes: Conduct adversarial load testing and sponge example testing to validate resource consumption limits hold under attack conditions

mappings.control_name: Assess, prioritise, and remediate — availability remediation
mappings.notes: Define remediation procedures for availability incidents including model rollback, rate limit tightening, and cost circuit breakers

mappings.control_name: Review for security vulnerabilities — resource handling
mappings.notes: Include resource exhaustion scenarios in pre-release security reviews; verify that token limits and rate limits are enforced by design
Enforce boundary protection on all AI inference endpoints — rate limiting, token quotas, and cost circuit breakers at the network and application boundary
Monitor AI inference services for resource consumption anomalies — token spikes, latency degradation, cost overruns; alert and auto-mitigate on threshold breach
Restrict AI inference services to minimum necessary compute, memory, and token budgets; disable unused model capabilities and endpoints
Log resource consumption per inference request — tokens, latency, cost; enable detection and forensic analysis of DoS patterns
Implement rate limiting, token quotas, and cost circuit breakers on AI inference services to prevent resource exhaustion and denial of service
Monitor AI inference services for resource consumption anomalies; alert on token spikes, latency degradation, and cost overruns
Maintain backup model deployments, checkpoint restoration capability, and fallback inference paths for AI services supporting critical financial functions

mappings.control_name: Response and Recovery — DoS incident response
mappings.notes: Define response and recovery procedures for AI denial of service events; include automated throttling, failover, and service restoration
